EPISODE_NARRATIVE: During the afternoon of July 17th, a severe thunderstorm formed over southeast Oregon.  This storm produced wind gusts of 70 to 80 mph in Malheur County before it moved into southwest Idaho and continued to produce severe weather.

During the afternoon of July 17th, a severe thunderstorm formed over southeast Oregon.  This storm produced wind gusts of 70 to 80 mph in Malheur County before it moved into southwest Idaho and continued to produce severe weather.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: REPORTED BY SPOTTER TRAVELLING ON HWY 58.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: During the evening of July 21st a dry thunderstorm produced wind damage in the Baker County town of Keating.  Spotter reports indicated trees and power lines down along highway 203 and a tree down on a trailer.  Dry lightning from this storm started 4 wildfires in Harney and Malheur Counties.  The largest fire burned 1200 acres near the town of Juntura before being brought under control on the evening of the 22nd..  Little damage was reported from these range fires.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: FOREST SERVICE PERSONNEL REPORTED TWO FUNNEL CLOUDS NEAR SILVER LAKE.
REPORTED BY FOREST SERVICE PERSONNEL
A thunderstorm produced 7/8 inch hail at Owyhee Dam in Malheur County.

A weather spotter at Prineville Resevoir State Park reported dime sized hail and up to an inch of rain in 30 minutes.  Another spotter three miles north of Prineville Resevoir reported marble sized hail.
A tornado was seen skipping along an unpopulated rural area 15 miles west of Albany by a pilot. No other information is available.
Hail up to one inch damaged some recreational vehicles around Crane Prairie Reservoir.  The hail put holes in some awnings and broke a few skylights out.
A lightning strike touched off a one and a half acre fire on a residential hillside in Prineville.  Homeowners were able to fight the fire with garden hoses.  No homes were damaged.
Weather spotters reported hail between 0.5 and 1.25 inches.
Weather spotters reported hail between .5 and .75 of an inch...covering the ground in places.  One spotter reported 0.70 of an inch of rain in twenty minutes.
Oregon Port of Entry reported a thunderstorm wind gust to 60 mph.
Thunderstorm wind gusted to 59 mph at the Pendleton Airport.
A thunderstorm produced hail up to 3/4 inch.
A weather spotter reported 3/4 inch hail.
Forest Service reported that a road washed out near Izee.
A lightning strike started a 25 acre fire on Black Crater about  seven miles west of Sisters. No structures were threatened.
A lightning strike knocked out a pump at a wastewater treatment plant causing between 50,000 and 70,000 gallons of wastewater to spill into Indian Ford Creek.
Heavy rain in Madras caused flooding of some streets and businesses.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A four car crash sent more than a dozen cars sliding into each other on highway 26.  Five people were injured.

A warm southwest flow pushed temperatures into the 60s  New Years Eve Day with the freezing lifting to above 10,000 ft.  Melting snow and copious amount of rain drove rivers and streams well above flood stageacross Southern Oregon New Years Day with wide spread flooding through Jan 3. The basin average storm total rainfall for the New Years Day storm averaged 2-4 inches with rainfall amounts excess 8 inches. Strong winds spread across Southern Oregon with Cape Blanco reporting 100+ mph winds...spotters in Klamath and Lake counties reported winds of 35-55 mph and gusts to 70 mph  New Years Day.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Another surge of moisture brought an additional 11 inches of snow to Mt. Hood Meadows and Bennett Pass, 9 inches to Timberline, and 7 inches at Government Camp.

Highway 395 south of Hermiston was closed for a few hours when high wind and blowing dust reduced visibility to less than 50 feet.  The dust is believed to have played a role in a minor accident on the highway.

Lightning blew up a large transformer and a small natural gas pipeline east of Bend.  About 14,000 customers were without power for an hour.  Twentyfive customers were without gas untill a section of the pipe was replaced.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: During the evening of June 17, a severe thunderstorm developed over southeastern Oregon. This thunderstorm produced numerous downbursts of wind speeds from 70 to near 100 mph, and one confirmed tornado as it moved northeast and crossed into Idaho around 5:10 pm MDT. Downburst winds destroyed a seed company building.  An estimated 8,700 acres of crops were damaged by the winds and hail. Total damage estimates reached 3 million dollars in Malheur County. An F-0 tornado touched down north of Ontario. The twister blew a pick-up truck off the road before dissipating as the parent thunderstorm moved across the Snake River into Idaho.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds at Netarts gusted to 70 mph, Tillamook 67 mph, and Cannon Beach 65 mph.
Winds at Cannon Beach gusted to 60 mph.

Strong winds associated with a Pacific storm lashed the north coast in the early evening. At 610 PM winds gusted to 58 mph at Astoria airport, flipping over a small airplane on the ground. Trees in the Astoria area were also reported being blown down. At 7 PM winds gusted to 78 mph at Netarts and Cannon Beach, and 75 mph at Warrenton.
March went out like a lion. A strong Pacific storm brought high winds to the coast again. At 130 PM winds gusted to 62 mph at Netarts.

Hail from 0.75 to 1.75 inches fell in Bend.
Heavy rain and lightning strikes knocked out power from Opal City to Madras.  Some phone service was also down for a while.
Lightning struck electrical equipment near an Elgin substation knocking out power to 1,100 customers.  Lightning also hit a pole north of Elgin spliting it in half and blew fuses on the line.
Winds at Portland International Airport gusted to 58 mph at 1130 AM.

At 1030 AM winds gusting to 45 mph and swell 3 to 4 feet capsized a boat on Fern Ridge reservoir. The sole occupant of the boat drowned, and his body was not recovered until April. Winds gusted to 60 mph at Veneta shortly after the boat capsized.
March came in like a lion. A strong Pacific storm caused south winds that gusted to 78 mph at Netarts.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall over Northwest Oregon during the last week of December caused 16 rivers in Northwestern Oregon to flood. Dozens of homes were flooded on various rivers and numerous highways were inundated throughout the region.

On the afternoon of April  30, severe thunderstorms erupted over Southeastern Oregon.  These thunderstorms produced two confirmed  weak tornadoes near Ontario. The strongest twister touched down near the hamlet of Cairo Junction .  It  destroyed a metal construction barn, ripped up some irrigation pipe, broke windows and lifted a two thousand pound camper  and carried it 50 feet. The second tornado touched down at  Farewell Bend State Park in Baker County. It did  minimal damage to a fence before dissipating  as it crossed the Snake River.   Weather spotters also reported at least two funnel clouds  which  did not reach the ground  in the area between Ontario and Nyssa Oregon.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: The first snow storm of the season dropped up to four inches of snow in the Pendleton area.  In Hermiston freezing rain fell before and after about two inches of snow complicating driving conditions.  An inch and a half of snow fell in Milton-Freewater with no freezing rain.  Several flights were canceled at the Eastern Oregon Regional Airport in Pendleton because of the snow and freezing rain.  A school bus slid into a power pole.  None of the 35 students on the bus were injured and they walked the rest of the way to school.

A weak tornado touched down in Garibaldi and moved a mobile home 2 feet in the air and dropped it about 3 feet off of it's foundation.
A weak tornado briefly touched down in rural Polk county. A  house was slightly damaged by a falling tree.

The first strong storm of the season caused wind gusts to 70 mph at Cannon Beach 58 mph at Tillamook and 61 mph at Seaside on the north coast and gusts to 62 mph at Sea Lion Caves on the central coast.

Strong wind caused power outages to about 350 homes in the La Grande and Perry areas when tree limbs blew into power lines.  Wind gusts to 47 mph  in Joseph caused a number of homes to lose  power for an hour.
A weak tornado briefly touched down in rural Polk County. Some farms sustained minor crop damage.

High wind in Bend knocked a large tree onto power lines then onto two parked cars at around 1pm .  No one was hurt and no power was lost.  A tall tree also fell across highway 20 east of Sisters  causing some traffic problems.
A tornado briefly touched down in the Eagle Crest Resort along Cline Falls Highway north of Bend.  Some juniper trees were blown down,  fences rolled up,  and about 10 acres of hay were ripped up.
Fog shrouded the Eastern Oregon Regional Airport causing flights to be canceled.
A tornado briefly touched down over Cascade Head around 830 AM.
Winds gusted to 58 mph at Seaside during a Pacific storm.
The last notable Pacific storm of the month produced winds that gusted to 58 mph at the Columbia River Bar.
Another tornado was reported near Macleay at 1105 am. No damages reported.
A resident of Pacific City reported seeing a funnel clouod over the ocean touch down briefly over the ocean and again inland. No damages were reported.
A  tornado caused minor damage to fences windows and trees in a rural subdivision near Turner.

Fog and ice caused at least 5 traffic accidents on Interstate 84 between between milepost 223 and 229.   On Christmas Eve a  car ran into the back of a parked semi-truck.  The four occupants of the car were treated for minor injuries and released.  On  Christmas day at least four cars slid off the road and crashed into snow banks.  No one was injured.
Fog caused several minor traffic accidents near Stanfield and Hermiston.
A downburst caused minor damage to a Westfall, Oregon homestead by ripping shingles off of a roof and peeling pieces of siding off of a  barn.

CZ_NAME: SE MULTNOMAH/C & S HOOD RIVER/E CLAKAMAS/E MARION/E LINN/E LANE
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow in the Cascades accompanied the months strongest storm. Timberline Lodge on Mt. Hood in the Northern Oregon cascades reported 9 inches of new snow from this storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A powerful Pacific storm brought high winds to the Oregon coast. The highest wind speeds reported included sustained winds of 55 mph with gusts to 89 mph at Florence in Lane county, sustained 60 mph with gusts to 80 mph at Tillamook in Tillamook county, sustained 60 mph gusting to 80 mph at Netarts in Clatsop county, and sustained 51 mph gusting to 80 mph in Newport in Lincoln county.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds from a strong Pacific storm gusted to 43 mph in Corvallis, and a strong gust blew a 1947 Taylor airplane from its moorings at Albany Municipal airport. The aircraft hit several parked cars before crashing into a fence, causing extensive damage to the aircraft.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The last major storm of 1997 brought high winds to the Northern and Central Oregon coast. Maximum reported wind speeds included sustained 58 mph gusting to 78 mph at Florence and sustained 58 mph gusting to 70 mph at Sea Lion Caves, both in Lane county, gusts to 70 mph at Netarts and 69 mph at Garibaldi in Tillamook couty, sustained 54 mph gusting to 66 mph at Newport in Lincoln county, and a gust to 65 mph at Cannon Beach in Clatsop county.
